title: Aureline AI model registry: model identity, capability-set, versioning, deprecation lane, quota family, context-window, and per-feature posture vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the model_registry_entry_record and model_registry_audit_event_record frozen in docs/ai/provider_model_registry_contract.md. Every model the product may cite — whether served locally from a signed pack, through a BYOK vendor or self-hosted endpoint, through an enterprise gateway, or from a vendor-hosted first-party-managed route — resolves to one governed row on this boundary. The model identity (model_entry_id, model_family_label, model_variant_label, model_capability_version) is stable across loci so a local-only pack row and a hosted-route row referring to the same underlying model family can be correlated mechanically by reviewers and support. Raw URLs, raw vendor-side internal ids, raw weight hashes, raw prompt-tokeniser bodies, raw pack bytes, raw provider payloads, and raw credential bodies never cross this boundary; rows carry opaque refs and structured fields only. The schema composes with (and does not replace) schemas/ai/provider_registry.schema.json (execution locus, transport, retention stance, quota family, data-class allowlist are authored on the provider entry row that serves the model; the model row declares its capabilities and the subset of posture axes the model itself narrows), schemas/ai/context_assembly.schema.json (supported_feature_class, cost_visibility_class, redaction_class), and schemas/release/assurance_claim.schema.json (deprecation-lane posture). The eventual provider-arbitration crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new model_capability_class, capability_posture_class, tokeniser_posture_class, context_window_class, deprecation_lane_class, model_lifecycle_state_class, audit_event_id, or denial_reason is additive-minor and requires a model_registry_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

model_registry_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this AI model-registry vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / evidence packets. Raw URLs, raw vendor-side internal ids, raw weight hashes, raw prompt-tokeniser bodies, raw pack bytes, and raw credential bodies never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show in model-picker readout, admin console, and support-export review surfaces.

supported_feature_class.description: Supported-feature-class vocabulary re-exported from schemas/ai/provider_registry.schema.json without modification. The intersection of the model row's supported_feature_classes and the serving provider's supported_feature_classes is what an invocation may request; a request outside the intersection denies with provider_exceeds_feature_class_policy on the provider side.

capability_posture_class.description: Posture class for a single capability claim on the model row. first_party_verified_capability = Aureline's parity lab has measured the capability on a fixed test corpus; vendor_attested_capability = only the vendor's documentation attests it; community_reported_capability = community benchmarks attest it; parity_tested_capability = the parity harness has recorded it but not first-party-verified yet; unverified_capability = admitted with an explicit unverified chip; deprecated_capability / withdrawn_capability = the capability is being retired.

tokeniser_posture_class.description: Tokeniser posture the assembly / route / spend layers use when estimating size. vendor_opaque_tokeniser_* modes force the byte_budget_hint to declare opaque_provider_units per schemas/ai/context_assembly.schema.json; tokeniser_unknown_unverified forces a typed fabrication-warning chip at the budget chip.

context_window_class.description: Coarse context-window size bucket for the model-picker readout. Never the exact vendor-advertised number; the number is subject to vendor-side change and the chip renders the bucket verbatim. window_unknown_unverified forces an unverified chip at the point of intent.

deprecation_lane_class.description: Deprecation-lane vocabulary naming where this model entry sits relative to the product's supported-model lane. soft_deprecated_still_available = visible but chip renders a deprecation warning; hard_deprecated_denies_new_sessions = existing sessions run, new sessions deny with provider_class_unresolved / model_identity_mismatch_on_evidence (surfaced as deprecation); withdrawn_denies_all_invocation = all invocation denies; vendor_withdrew_without_notice = the vendor silently retired the model and this row records the narrower default.

model_lifecycle_state_class.description: Lifecycle state of the model entry. Composes with deprecation_lane_class: the lane is the runtime-facing contract (admit / deny); the state is the product-facing announcement (preview / GA / LTS / withdrawn). A row whose state is withdrawn MUST carry deprecation_lane_class in {withdrawn_denies_all_invocation, vendor_withdrew_without_notice}.

determinism_posture_class.description: Determinism posture the model row advertises for the replay and parity lanes. deterministic_replay_from_evidence_packet is the strongest mode: a prior evidence packet's seed / temperature / top-p / sampler / tokeniser posture is replayable; weaker modes surface the narrower default at the replay chip.

instruction_following_profile_class.description: Instruction-following profile the model advertises. base_model_completion_only and embedding_only_not_instruction_tuned are admitted as first-class citizens so a code-completion pack or embeddings pack does not have to masquerade as chat-tuned.

modality_class.description: High-level modality class the model supports. multimodal_vendor_opaque is admitted when the vendor exposes multimodal routing without publishing which modalities are served by which internal model.

quota_family_class.description: Quota-family class re-exported from schemas/ai/provider_registry.schema.json without modification. The model row declares the quota families the model itself narrows (for example, a vision-capable model on a vendor route may narrow per_user_byok_vendor_quota to paid_tier_metered).

cost_visibility_class.description: Cost-visibility class re-exported from schemas/ai/context_assembly.schema.json without modification. undisclosed_by_provider is admitted only when the provider explicitly does not expose cost metadata; surfaces render the state verbatim.

redaction_class.description: Redaction class re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011).

policy_context.description: Policy context the row was minted under. A policy-epoch roll invalidates prior model-picker readouts and forces re-disclosure before the next invocation.

audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event ids on the ai_model_registry audit stream.

denial_reason.description: Frozen denial-reason vocabulary. Denials fail closed.

parity_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to a schemas/qe/public_proof_packet.schema.json row (or equivalent parity-lab record) backing the capability. Required (non-empty) when capability_posture_class is first_party_verified_capability or parity_tested_capability; empty string otherwise.

allOf.description: Verified / parity-tested capabilities MUST cite a parity packet ref.

display_label.description: Short reviewer-visible label. Never the raw vendor-internal id; never a raw URL or raw weight hash.

model_family_label.description: Short reviewer-visible family label the model picker renders.

model_variant_label.description: Short reviewer-visible variant label (parameter count / quantisation / size class).

model_capability_version.description: Stable capability-version string the row advertises for parity / replay / evidence packet correlation. Bumped when the underlying capability set changes in a way replay consumers must observe.

capability_claims.description: One claim per advertised feature class, each naming the posture (first-party verified, vendor attested, community reported, parity tested, unverified, deprecated, withdrawn).

served_by_provider_entry_refs.description: Opaque refs to ai_provider_registry_entry_record rows that may serve this model. The same model row MAY be referenced by a local-pack-serving provider row, a BYOK vendor row, an enterprise-gateway row, and a vendor-hosted row simultaneously so reviewers can correlate identity across loci.

served_by_local_model_pack_refs.description: Opaque refs to local_model_pack_entry_record rows (schemas/ai/provider_registry.schema.json) this model may be served from. Empty when the model is only served through remote loci.

replacement_model_entry_ref.description: Opaque ref to the replacement model entry when lifecycle state is deprecated_with_replacement. Empty string otherwise.

allOf.description: Every claim's feature_class MUST appear in supported_feature_classes.
A deprecated_with_replacement lifecycle MUST carry a non-empty replacement_model_entry_ref.
Withdrawn state MUST carry withdrawn_denies_all_invocation or vendor_withdrew_without_notice.

model_registry_entry_record.description: One governed row per model entry. The row is the identity every invocation cites on its ai_context_assembly_record, ai_route_receipt_record, ai_spend_receipt_record, and ai_evidence_packet_record so a local-pack-served instance and a hosted-route-served instance of the same model family can be correlated mechanically by reviewers.

denial_reason.description: Optional denial_reason string from the denial_reason enum. Empty string for non-deny events.

model_registry_audit_event_record.description: Observable event on the ai_model_registry audit stream.
